149	fn main() -> Result<()> {
150	    let cli = Cli::parse();
151	
152	    let style = match cli.style {
153	        StyleArg::Cjk => Style::Cjk,
154	        StyleArg::Ascii => Style::Ascii,
155	    };
156	    let observer = match cli.observer {
157	        SideArg::Red => Side::RED,
158	        SideArg::Black => Side::BLACK,
159	    };
160	    let use_color = !cli.no_color;
161	
162	    let show_confetti = !cli.no_confetti;
163	    let show_check_banner = !cli.no_check_banner;
164	    let captured_sort = match cli.captured_sort {
165	        CapturedSortArg::Time => CapturedSort::Time,
166	        CapturedSortArg::Rank => CapturedSort::Rank,
167	    };
168	
169	    let mut app = if let Some(host) = cli.lobby.as_deref() {
170	        let url = normalize_lobby_url(host).map_err(|e| anyhow!(e))?;
171	        // Strip the /lobby suffix before storing as `host` so the lobby
172	        // can re-derive `/ws/<id>` for joins.
173	        let host = url.trim_end_matches("/lobby").to_string();
174	        AppState::new_lobby(host, style, use_color, observer)
175	    } else if let Some(url) = cli.connect.as_deref() {
176	        let mut url = normalize_connect_url(url).map_err(|e| anyhow!(e))?;
177	        if let Some(pw) = cli.password.as_deref() {
178	            let sep = if url.contains('?') { '&' } else { '?' };
179	            url = format!("{url}{sep}password={}", crate::url::urlencode(pw));
180	        }
181	        AppState::new_net(url, style, use_color)
182	    } else {
183	        match &cli.cmd {
184	            None => AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer),
185	            Some(Cmd::Xiangqi { strict }) => {
186	                let rules = if *strict { RuleSet::xiangqi() } else { RuleSet::xiangqi_casual() };
187	                AppState::new_game(rules, style, use_color, observer)
188	            }
189	            Some(Cmd::Banqi { preset, house, seed }) => {
190	                let rules = build_banqi_rules(preset.as_ref(), house.as_deref(), *seed)?;
191	                AppState::new_game(rules, style, use_color, observer)
192	            }
193	        }
194	    };
195	
196	    app.show_confetti = show_confetti;
197	    app.show_check_banner = show_check_banner;
198	    app.captured_sort = captured_sort;
199	    run(app)
200	}
201	
202	fn build_banqi_rules(
203	    preset: Option<&PresetArg>,
204	    house: Option<&str>,
205	    seed: Option<u64>,
206	) -> Result<RuleSet> {
207	    let mut flags = HouseRules::empty();
208	    if let Some(p) = preset {
209	        flags |= match p {
210	            PresetArg::Purist => chess_core::rules::PRESET_PURIST,
211	            PresetArg::Taiwan => chess_core::rules::PRESET_TAIWAN,
212	            PresetArg::Aggressive => chess_core::rules::PRESET_AGGRESSIVE,
213	        };
214	    }
215	    if let Some(s) = house {
216	        for tok in s.split(',') {
217	            flags |= match tok.trim() {
218	                "chain" => HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE,
219	                "dark" | "dark-chain" | "dark-capture" => HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE,
220	                "dark-trade" | "trade" => HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE,
221	                "rush" | "chariot-rush" => HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH,
222	                "horse" | "horse-diagonal" | "diag" => HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL,
223	                "cannon-fast" | "fast-cannon" => HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE,
224	                other => return Err(anyhow!("unknown house rule: {other}")),
225	            };
226	        }
227	    }
228	    let flags = chess_core::rules::house::normalize(flags);
229	    Ok(match seed {
230	        Some(s) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(flags, s),
231	        None => RuleSet::banqi(flags),
232	    })
233	}
234	
235	fn run(mut app: AppState) -> Result<()> {
236	    let mut terminal = setup_terminal()?;
237	    let result = run_loop(&mut terminal, &mut app);
238	    teardown_terminal(&mut terminal).ok();
239	    result
240	}
241	
242	fn setup_terminal() -> Result<Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>> {
243	    enable_raw_mode().context("enable raw mode")?;
244	    let mut stdout = io::stdout();
245	    execute!(stdout, EnterAlternateScreen, crossterm::event::EnableMouseCapture)
246	        .context("enter alt screen")?;
247	    let backend = CrosstermBackend::new(stdout);
248	    let terminal = Terminal::new(backend).context("init terminal")?;
249	    Ok(terminal)
250	}
251	
252	fn teardown_terminal(terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>) -> Result<()> {
253	    disable_raw_mode().ok();
254	    execute!(terminal.backend_mut(), LeaveAlternateScreen, crossterm::event::DisableMouseCapture)
255	        .ok();
256	    terminal.show_cursor().ok();
257	    Ok(())
258	}
259	
260	fn run_loop(terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>, app: &mut AppState) -> Result<()> {
261	    loop {
262	        // Drain ws events first so the next draw shows server-pushed state.
263	        // No-op outside Net mode.
264	        app.tick_net();
265	
266	        terminal.draw(|frame| ui::draw(frame, app))?;
267	        if app.should_quit {
268	            return Ok(());
269	        }
270	
271	        // Shorter poll in Net / Lobby modes keeps server pushes feeling
272	        // snappy (we redraw at most every poll interval). 200ms in
273	        // single-process modes is fine — no async source to drain. While
274	        // a confetti burst is active (or pending), drop to ~20fps so the
275	        // particles animate smoothly.
276	        let animating = app.confetti_anim.is_some() || app.confetti_pending;
277	        let poll_ms = if animating {
278	            50
279	        } else if matches!(app.screen, app::Screen::Net(_) | app::Screen::Lobby(_)) {
280	            60
281	        } else {
282	            200
283	        };
284	        if event::poll(Duration::from_millis(poll_ms))? {
285	            let mode = app.input_mode();
286	            let quit_confirm = app.quit_confirm_open;
287	            let action = match event::read()? {
288	                Event::Key(k) if k.kind == event::KeyEventKind::Press => {
289	                    from_key(k, mode, quit_confirm)
290	                }
291	                Event::Mouse(m) => from_mouse(m),
292	                Event::Resize(_, _) => Action::None,
293	                _ => Action::None,
294	            };
295	            app.dispatch(action);
296	        }
297	    }
298	}
299
```

```text
1	//! Keyboard / mouse events → `Action`. Pure mapping; no state mutation.
2	
3	use crossterm::event::{KeyCode, KeyEvent, KeyModifiers, MouseButton, MouseEvent, MouseEventKind};
4	
5	/// Where the user's focus is right now. Drives the keymap branch in
6	/// [`from_key`]. `Game` covers both local hot-seat (`Screen::Game`) and
7	/// online (`Screen::Net`) play.
8	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
9	pub enum InputMode {
10	    Picker,
11	    Lobby,
12	    Text,
13	    Game,
14	}
15	
16	/// Flavor of the coordinate-input prompt opened from `Game` mode.
17	/// `Instant` = pure text buffer, no board feedback.
18	/// `Live` = each keystroke re-parses; valid square prefixes update the
19	/// `selected` highlight, and a complete `from+to` move also moves the
20	/// cursor onto the destination square as a preview.
21	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
22	pub enum CoordKind {
23	    Instant,
24	    Live,
25	}
26	
27	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
28	pub enum Action {
29	    None,
30	    Quit,
31	    /// Confirm an open quit-confirm dialog (user pressed y/Y).
32	    ConfirmYes,
33	    /// Cancel an open quit-confirm dialog (user pressed anything else).
34	    ConfirmNo,
35	    HelpToggle,
36	    RulesToggle,
37	    NewGame,
38	    /// "Back one screen" — Esc semantics outside game/picker. The
39	    /// dispatcher decides what to back into per screen.
40	    Back,
41	    /// Toggle the captured-pieces panel sort order (Time ↔ Rank).
42	    /// Bound to `g` ("graveyard") in `Game` mode; Picker / Lobby ignore.
43	    CapturedSortToggle,
44	    // Picker / Lobby (list-cursor)
45	    PickerUp,
46	    PickerDown,
47	    PickerSelect,
48	    // Lobby
49	    LobbyCreate,
50	    LobbyRefresh,
51	    /// Spectate the highlighted room (`?role=spectator`).
52	    LobbyWatch,
53	    // Text input (HostPrompt / CreateRoom)
54	    TextInput(char),
55	    TextBackspace,
56	    FocusNext,
57	    FocusPrev,
58	    /// Submit a text-input form (Enter while in `Text` mode).
59	    Submit,
60	    // Game
61	    CursorUp,
62	    CursorDown,
63	    CursorLeft,
64	    CursorRight,
65	    SelectOrCommit,
66	    Cancel,
67	    Undo,
68	    Flip,
69	    /// Open the chat input editor (Net mode, players only).
70	    ChatStart,
71	    /// Open the coordinate-input prompt (Game / Net, players only). The
72	    /// kind picks instant-vs-live preview behaviour.
73	    CoordStart(CoordKind),
74	    /// Mouse click in terminal coords. The app resolves it against the board
75	    /// rect captured by the most recent draw().
76	    Click {
77	        term_col: u16,
78	        term_row: u16,
79	    },
80	}
81	
82	pub fn from_key(ev: KeyEvent, mode: InputMode, quit_confirm_open: bool) -> Action {
83	    // Quit-confirm dialog hijacks input: y/Y confirms, anything else cancels.
84	    if quit_confirm_open {
85	        return match ev.code {
86	            KeyCode::Char('y') | KeyCode::Char('Y') => Action::ConfirmYes,
87	            _ => Action::ConfirmNo,
88	        };
89	    }
90	    if ev.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) && matches!(ev.code, KeyCode::Char('c')) {
91	        return Action::Quit;
92	    }
93	    match mode {
94	        InputMode::Picker => match ev.code {
95	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::PickerUp,
96	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::PickerDown,
97	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::PickerSelect,
98	            KeyCode::Char('q') => Action::Quit,
99	            KeyCode::Char('?') => Action::HelpToggle,
100	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Quit,
101	            _ => Action::None,
102	        },
103	        InputMode::Lobby => match ev.code {
104	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::PickerUp,
105	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::PickerDown,
106	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::PickerSelect,
107	            KeyCode::Char('c') => Action::LobbyCreate,
108	            KeyCode::Char('r') => Action::LobbyRefresh,
109	            KeyCode::Char('w') => Action::LobbyWatch,
110	            KeyCode::Char('q') => Action::Quit,
111	            KeyCode::Char('?') => Action::HelpToggle,
112	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Back,
113	            _ => Action::None,
114	        },
115	        InputMode::Text => match ev.code {
116	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Back,
117	            KeyCode::Enter => Action::Submit,
118	            KeyCode::Backspace => Action::TextBackspace,
119	            KeyCode::Tab => Action::FocusNext,
120	            KeyCode::BackTab => Action::FocusPrev,
121	            KeyCode::Char(c) => Action::TextInput(c),
122	            _ => Action::None,
123	        },
124	        InputMode::Game => match ev.code {
125	            KeyCode::Char('q') => Action::Quit,
126	            KeyCode::Char('?') => Action::HelpToggle,
127	            KeyCode::Char('r') => Action::RulesToggle,
128	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::CursorUp,
129	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::CursorDown,
130	            KeyCode::Left | KeyCode::Char('h') => Action::CursorLeft,
131	            KeyCode::Right | KeyCode::Char('l') => Action::CursorRight,
132	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::SelectOrCommit,
133	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Cancel,
134	            KeyCode::Char('u') => Action::Undo,
135	            KeyCode::Char('f') => Action::Flip,
136	            KeyCode::Char('n') => Action::NewGame,
137	            KeyCode::Char('g') => Action::CapturedSortToggle,
138	            KeyCode::Char('t') => Action::ChatStart,
139	            KeyCode::Char(':') => Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Instant),
140	            KeyCode::Char('m') => Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Live),
141	            _ => Action::None,
142	        },
143	    }
144	}
145	
146	pub fn from_mouse(ev: MouseEvent) -> Action {
147	    match ev.kind {
148	        MouseEventKind::Down(MouseButton::Left) => {
149	            Action::Click { term_col: ev.column, term_row: ev.row }
150	        }
151	        _ => Action::None,
152	    }
153	}
154
```
</details></tool-use>

2	
3	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
4	use chess_core::coord::Square;
5	use chess_core::piece::{PieceKind, Side};
6	use chess_core::state::{GameStatus, WinReason};

12	use ratatui::widgets::{Block, Borders, Clear, Paragraph, Wrap};
13	use ratatui::Frame;

15	use crate::app::{
16	    self, AppState, CapturedSort, CreateRoomField, CreateRoomView, GameView, HostPromptView,
17	    LobbyView, NetRole, NetView, PickerEntry, PickerView, RectPx, Screen,

28	
29	pub fn draw(frame: &mut Frame, app: &mut AppState) {
30	    let area = frame.area();
31	    let app_style = app.style;
32	    match &app.screen {
33	        Screen::Picker(p) => draw_picker(frame, area, p, app_style),
34	        Screen::Game(_) => draw_game(frame, area, app),
35	        Screen::Net(_) => draw_net(frame, area, app),
36	        Screen::HostPrompt(h) => draw_host_prompt(frame, area, h, app.help_open),
37	        Screen::Lobby(l) => draw_lobby(frame, area, l, app.help_open),
38	        Screen::CreateRoom(c) => draw_create_room(frame, area, c),
39	    }
40	
41	    if app.rules_open {
42	        draw_rules_overlay(frame, area, app.style);
43	    }
44	    if app.quit_confirm_open {
45	        draw_quit_confirm_overlay(frame, area);
46	    }
47	}
48	
49	fn draw_picker(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, picker: &PickerView, style: Style) {
50	    let mut lines: Vec<Line> = Vec::new();
51	
52	    // Big "CHESS TUI" ASCII banner — only when the terminal is wide enough.
53	    // Narrow terminals (less than the banner width + a little padding) fall
54	    // back to the original single-line title so the picker still fits.
55	    let title_art = banner::art(BannerKind::AppTitle, style);
56	    let banner_w = banner::max_width(title_art);
57	    if (area.width as usize) >= banner_w + 4 {
58	        for row in title_art {
59	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
60	                row.to_string(),
61	                TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
62	            )));
63	        }
64	    } else {
65	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
66	            "chess-tui",
67	            TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
68	        )));
69	    }
70	    lines.push(Line::from(""));
71	    lines.push(Line::from(Span::raw("Choose a variant. Arrow keys + Enter; q to quit.")));
72	    lines.push(Line::from(""));
73	    for (i, entry) in PickerEntry::ALL.iter().enumerate() {
74	        let prefix = if i == picker.cursor { "▶ " } else { "  " };
75	        let row_style = if i == picker.cursor {
76	            TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)
77	        } else {
78	            TuiStyle::default()
79	        };
80	        lines.push(Line::from(vec![Span::raw(prefix), Span::styled(entry.label(), row_style)]));
81	    }
82	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Welcome ");
83	    let para = Paragraph::new(lines).block(block).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
84	    frame.render_widget(para, area);
85	}
86	
87	fn draw_game(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, app: &mut AppState) {
88	    let chunks = Layout::default()
89	        .direction(Direction::Horizontal)
90	        .constraints([Constraint::Min(40), Constraint::Length(36)])
91	        .split(area);
92	
93	    let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen else {
94	        return;
95	    };
96	    let g_ref: &GameView = g.as_ref();
97	    let observer = app.observer;
98	    let style = app.style;
99	    let use_color = app.use_color;
100	    let help_open = app.help_open;
101	    let show_check_banner = app.show_check_banner;
102	
103	    let view = PlayerView::project(&g_ref.state, g_ref.state.side_to_move);
104	    // Engine 連吃 chain_lock takes precedence as the "effective selection"
105	    // so the locked piece + its legal next-hops light up automatically —
106	    // the user doesn't have to re-click their attacker between hops.
107	    let effective_selected = view.chain_lock.or(g_ref.selected);
108	    let mut rect = app.board_rect;
109	    draw_board(
110	        frame,
111	        chunks[0],
112	        observer,
113	        style,
114	        use_color,
115	        &view,
116	        g_ref.cursor,
117	        effective_selected,
118	        &mut rect,
119	    );
120	    app.board_rect = rect;
121	    let captured_sort = app.captured_sort;
122	    draw_sidebar(
123	        frame,
124	        chunks[1],
125	        g_ref,
126	        &view,
127	        style,
128	        help_open,
129	        show_check_banner,
130	        captured_sort,
131	    );
132	
133	    // Confetti + endgame banner. The board area is `chunks[0]`. We do this
134	    // after the sidebar render so the layer paints on top of the board only,
135	    // and after we've taken the `&app.screen` immutable borrow above.
136	    let board_area = chunks[0];
137	    let endgame_kind = endgame_kind_local(&view.status, observer);
138	    render_confetti_and_banner(frame, board_area, app, endgame_kind, style, use_color);
139	}
140	
141	fn draw_net(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, app: &mut AppState) {
142	    let chunks = Layout::default()
143	        .direction(Direction::Horizontal)
144	        .constraints([Constraint::Min(40), Constraint::Length(36)])
145	        .split(area);
146	
147	    let Screen::Net(n) = &app.screen else {
148	        return;
149	    };
150	    let n_ref: &NetView = n.as_ref();
151	    let style = app.style;
152	    let use_color = app.use_color;
153	    let help_open = app.help_open;
154	    let show_check_banner = app.show_check_banner;
155	    let observer = n_ref.role.map(|r| r.observer()).unwrap_or(app.observer);
156	
157	    match n_ref.last_view.as_ref() {
158	        Some(view) => {
159	            let view_status = view.status;
160	            let role = n_ref.role;
161	            // Engine 連吃 chain_lock takes precedence as the "effective
162	            // selection" — chain-locked piece + next-hops auto-highlighted.
163	            let effective_selected = view.chain_lock.or(n_ref.selected);
164	            let mut rect = app.board_rect;

1	//! Hand-rolled block-letter ASCII art for end-of-game and check banners.
2	//!
3	//! No `figlet-rs` dep — the strings rendered are fixed (VICTORY, DEFEAT,
4	//! DRAW, CHECK, 將軍, the app title), so embedding the rasterised rows
5	//! is cheap and keeps the dependency graph clean. CJK style uses ░▒▓
6	//! block fills; ASCII style uses `#` so monochrome terminals still read.
7	//!
8	//! Each `&'static [&'static str]` is the rasterised letters as one row
9	//! per `&'static str`. Renderers join with `'\n'` and centre.
10	//!
11	//! Width characters are ASCII only (no CJK) so each `char` is one terminal
12	//! cell — `width()` is `s.chars().count()`.
13	//!
14	//! See `confetti.rs` for the matching particle effect and
15	//! `ui::draw_game_over_overlay` for the renderer that consumes both.
16	//!
17	//! IMPORTANT: keep all rows of a given variant the same width so that
18	//! centering is trivial; the renderer trusts row 0's width.
19	
20	use crate::glyph::Style;
21	
22	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
23	pub enum BannerKind {
24	    /// Player won (CJK: 勝利, ASCII: VICTORY).
25	    Victory,
26	    /// Player lost (DEFEAT / 敗北).
27	    Defeat,
28	    /// Game ended in a draw (DRAW / 和棋).
29	    Draw,
30	    /// Spectator-style neutral outcome — caller passes the winner side.
31	    Outcome(NeutralSide),
32	    /// chess-tui picker title.
33	    AppTitle,
34	}
35	
36	/// Spectator banner uses neutral wording ("RED WINS", not "VICTORY").
37	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
38	pub enum NeutralSide {
39	    Red,
40	    Black,
41	    Green,
42	}
43	
44	pub fn art(kind: BannerKind, style: Style) -> &'static [&'static str] {
45	    match (kind, style) {
46	        (BannerKind::Victory, Style::Cjk) => VICTORY_BLOCK,
47	        (BannerKind::Victory, Style::Ascii) => VICTORY_ASCII,
48	        (BannerKind::Defeat, Style::Cjk) => DEFEAT_BLOCK,
49	        (BannerKind::Defeat, Style::Ascii) => DEFEAT_ASCII,
50	        (BannerKind::Draw, Style::Cjk) => DRAW_BLOCK,
51	        (BannerKind::Draw, Style::Ascii) => DRAW_ASCII,
52	        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Red), Style::Cjk) => RED_WINS_BLOCK,
53	        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Red), Style::Ascii) => RED_WINS_ASCII,
54	        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Black), Style::Cjk) => BLACK_WINS_BLOCK,
55	        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Black), Style::Ascii) => BLACK_WINS_ASCII,
56	        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Green), Style::Cjk) => GREEN_WINS_BLOCK,
57	        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Green), Style::Ascii) => GREEN_WINS_ASCII,
58	        (BannerKind::AppTitle, Style::Cjk) => APP_TITLE_BLOCK,
59	        (BannerKind::AppTitle, Style::Ascii) => APP_TITLE_ASCII,
60	    }
61	}
62	
63	/// Maximum row width for a banner — useful for layout fits-check before
64	/// committing to the overlay (in narrow terminals we skip the overlay).
65	pub fn max_width(rows: &[&str]) -> usize {
66	    rows.iter().map(|r| r.chars().count()).max().unwrap_or(0)
67	}

1	//! Banqi move generation.
2	//!
3	//! Base rules (always emitted):
4	//! - `Reveal` for every face-down piece.
5	//! - 1-step orthogonal `Step` / `Capture` for face-up own pieces.
6	//! - Cannon `CannonJump` over exactly one screen (face-up or face-down).
7	//!
8	//! House-rule extensions:
9	//! - `CHAIN_CAPTURE`: emit `ChainCapture` for face-up consecutive captures
10	//!   along the same direction (length ≥ 2).
11	//! - `CHARIOT_RUSH`: chariot uses xiangqi-style multi-square ray for both
12	//!   slides and captures (rank-ignoring on captures with a gap).
13	//! - `DARK_CAPTURE` (`暗吃`): atomic reveal+capture against face-down
14	//!   targets. `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` (implies DARK_CAPTURE) makes
15	//!   rank-fail kill the attacker instead of probing in place.
16	//! - `HORSE_DIAGONAL` (`馬斜`): horse adds 4 diagonal one-step moves;
17	//!   diagonal captures ignore rank (any piece).
18	//!
19	//! Deferred (toggles ship as accepted-but-no-effect):
20	//! - `CANNON_FAST_MOVE`: cannon non-capturing slide.
21	
22	use smallvec::SmallVec;
23	
24	use crate::board::Board;
25	use crate::coord::{Direction, Square};
26	use crate::moves::{ChainHop, Move, MoveList};
27	use crate::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, Side};
28	use crate::rules::HouseRules;
29	use crate::state::GameState;
30	
31	pub fn generate(state: &GameState, out: &mut MoveList) {
32	    let house = state.rules.house;
33	    gen_reveals(state, out);
34	
35	    // After the first flip, the active seat may control the OPPOSITE
36	    // piece-color from its seat name. `current_color()` consults the
37	    // banqi side_assignment mapping; before the first reveal it falls
38	    // back to side_to_move (no constraint, since only Reveal moves are
39	    // generated above).
40	    let me = state.current_color();
41	    let board = &state.board;
42	
43	    for sq in board.squares() {
44	        let Some(pos) = board.get(sq) else { continue };
45	        if !pos.revealed || pos.piece.side != me {
46	            continue;
47	        }
48	        gen_for_face_up_piece(state, sq, pos.piece, house, out);
49	    }
50	}
51	
52	fn gen_reveals(state: &GameState, out: &mut MoveList) {
53	    // Banqi rule: ANY face-down piece can be flipped on a player's turn,
54	    // regardless of which side controls the seat. The first flip locks
55	    // the side assignment (handled in state::make_move).
56	    for sq in state.board.squares() {
57	        if let Some(pos) = state.board.get(sq) {
58	            if !pos.revealed {
59	                out.push(Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None });
60	            }
61	        }
62	    }
63	}
64	
65	fn gen_for_face_up_piece(
66	    state: &GameState,
67	    from: Square,
68	    piece: Piece,
69	    house: HouseRules,
70	    out: &mut MoveList,
71	) {
72	    let board = &state.board;
73	
74	    // 1-step orthogonal (and the 連吃 chains it seeds).
75	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
76	        let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
77	        match board.get(to) {
78	            None => {
79	                // Empty: 1-step slide.
80	                out.push(Move::Step { from, to });
81	            }
82	            Some(target) if !target.revealed => {
83	                // Face-down piece — blocks unless DARK_CAPTURE is on.
84	                // Cannon adjacent capture is illegal in standard banqi
85	                // (cannons capture only via jump-over-screen), so we
86	                // never emit a 1-step DarkCapture for a Cannon attacker
87	                // — its only DarkCapture path is the jump emitted in
88	                // `gen_cannon_jumps`.
89	                if house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE) && piece.kind != PieceKind::Cannon {
90	                    out.push(Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed: None, attacker: None });
91	                }
92	            }
93	            Some(target) if target.piece.side == piece.side => {
94	                // Own piece: blocked.
95	            }
96	            Some(target) => {
97	                if can_capture(piece.kind, target.piece.kind) {
98	                    out.push(Move::Capture { from, to, captured: target.piece });
99	                    if house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE) {
100	                        gen_chain_extensions(board, piece, from, to, dir, target.piece, out);
```

```text
1	//! Banqi house rules.
2	//!
3	//! Five independent toggles + three named presets. See `docs/rules/banqi-house.md`.
4	
5	use bitflags::bitflags;
6	
7	bitflags! {
8	    /// Toggleable banqi house rules. Combine with `|`.
9	    #[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Default)]
10	    #[derive(serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
11	    pub struct HouseRules: u32 {
12	        /// 連吃 — chain captures along the same line.
13	        const CHAIN_CAPTURE       = 1 << 0;
14	        /// 暗吃 — atomic reveal+capture of a face-down piece (probe variant:
15	        /// rank-fail leaves both pieces in place with target now revealed).
16	        const DARK_CAPTURE        = 1 << 1;
17	        /// 車衝 — chariot moves multi-square in a line.
18	        const CHARIOT_RUSH        = 1 << 2;
19	        /// 馬斜 — horse adds 4 diagonal one-step moves; diagonal captures
20	        /// ignore rank (any piece).
21	        const HORSE_DIAGONAL      = 1 << 3;
22	        /// 炮快移 — cannon non-capturing move slides any distance.
23	        const CANNON_FAST_MOVE    = 1 << 4;
24	        /// 暗吃 trade variant — on rank-fail the small attacker dies (is
25	        /// removed), target stays revealed. Implies DARK_CAPTURE.
26	        const DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE  = 1 << 5;
27	    }
28	}
29	
30	/// Classic banqi, no house rules.
31	pub const PRESET_PURIST: HouseRules = HouseRules::empty();
32	
33	/// Common Taiwanese house ruleset: chain captures + chariot rush.
34	pub const PRESET_TAIWAN: HouseRules = HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE.union(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH);
35	
36	/// Aggressive ruleset enabling chain, dark-capture, chariot rush, horse
37	/// diagonal.
38	pub const PRESET_AGGRESSIVE: HouseRules = HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE
39	    .union(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE)
40	    .union(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH)
41	    .union(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL);
42	
43	/// `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` implies `DARK_CAPTURE`. Returns the canonical form.
44	pub fn normalize(mut rules: HouseRules) -> HouseRules {
45	    if rules.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE) {
46	        rules.insert(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE);
47	    }
48	    rules
49	}
50	
51	#[cfg(test)]
52	mod tests {
53	    use super::*;
54	
55	    #[test]
56	    fn presets_are_distinct() {
57	        assert_ne!(PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_TAIWAN);
58	        assert_ne!(PRESET_TAIWAN, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE);
59	        assert_ne!(PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE);
60	    }
61	
62	    #[test]
63	    fn taiwan_preset_has_chain_and_rush() {
64	        assert!(PRESET_TAIWAN.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
65	        assert!(PRESET_TAIWAN.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
66	        assert!(!PRESET_TAIWAN.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
67	    }
68	
69	    #[test]
70	    fn dark_capture_trade_normalizes_to_include_dark_capture() {
71	        let r = normalize(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE);
72	        assert!(r.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
73	        assert!(r.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE));
74	    }
75	}

```text
1	//! Variant-slug parsing for `/local/:variant` plus URL-query rule encoding.
2	//!
3	//! Pure logic — native-testable. The picker page builds a query string from
4	//! its form state via [`build_local_query`]; the local page parses it back
5	//! into a [`RuleSet`] via [`parse_local_rules`] + [`build_rule_set`].
6	
7	use chess_core::rules::{
8	    house, HouseRules, RuleSet, Variant, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE, PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_TAIWAN,
9	};
10	
11	pub fn parse_variant_slug(slug: &str) -> Option<Variant> {
12	    match slug {
13	        "xiangqi" => Some(Variant::Xiangqi),
14	        "banqi" => Some(Variant::Banqi),
15	        "three-kingdom" => Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi),
16	        _ => None,
17	    }
18	}
19	
20	pub fn variant_slug(variant: Variant) -> &'static str {
21	    match variant {
22	        Variant::Xiangqi => "xiangqi",
23	        Variant::Banqi => "banqi",
24	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "three-kingdom",
25	    }
26	}
27	
28	/// URL-derived rule choices for `/local/:variant`. Used as an intermediate
29	/// between the picker form and the engine `RuleSet`.
30	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Default)]
31	pub struct LocalRulesParams {
32	    /// Xiangqi only. `false` (default) = casual / no self-check filter;
33	    /// `true` = standard rules where leaving your general in check is illegal.
34	    pub strict: bool,
35	    /// Banqi only. Bitflag set; `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` auto-implies
36	    /// `DARK_CAPTURE` after [`house::normalize`] in the parser.
37	    pub house: HouseRules,
38	    /// Banqi only. `None` = engine picks (non-deterministic on native;
39	    /// browser uses `getrandom` JS feature).
40	    pub seed: Option<u64>,
41	}
42	
43	/// Parse `?strict=1&house=chain,rush&preset=taiwan&seed=42` into a
44	/// [`LocalRulesParams`]. Unknown tokens are silently dropped — this is a
45	/// best-effort decoder for shareable URLs.
46	///
47	/// `house=` overrides `preset=` when both are present; the picker only
48	/// emits `house=` so this just covers manually-typed URLs.
49	pub fn parse_local_rules(get: impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String>) -> LocalRulesParams {
50	    let strict = matches!(get("strict").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true"));
51	    let preset = get("preset").as_deref().and_then(parse_preset_token);
52	    let house_csv = get("house").as_deref().map(parse_house_csv);
53	    let house = house_csv.or(preset).unwrap_or_else(HouseRules::empty);
54	    let seed = get("seed").and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());
55	    LocalRulesParams { strict, house: house::normalize(house), seed }
56	}
57	
58	/// Inverse of [`parse_local_rules`] — emits a stable canonical query string
59	/// (no leading `?`). Empty result means "use defaults" — the picker drops the
60	/// `?` entirely so `/local/xiangqi` with no query is the default-casual URL.
61	pub fn build_local_query(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
62	    let mut parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
63	    match variant {
64	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
65	            if params.strict {
66	                parts.push("strict=1".to_string());
67	            }
68	        }
69	        Variant::Banqi => {
70	            let csv = house_csv(params.house);
71	            if !csv.is_empty() {
72	                parts.push(format!("house={}", csv));
73	            }
74	            if let Some(seed) = params.seed {
75	                parts.push(format!("seed={}", seed));
76	            }
77	        }
78	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {}
79	    }
80	    parts.join("&")
81	}
82	
83	/// Build the full path for the picker's "Start" link given the current form.
84	/// Always returns `"/local/<slug>"` with the query string appended only when
85	/// non-empty.
86	pub fn build_local_href(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
87	    let q = build_local_query(variant, params);
88	    if q.is_empty() {
89	        format!("/local/{}", variant_slug(variant))
90	    } else {
91	        format!("/local/{}?{}", variant_slug(variant), q)
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	/// Convert parsed [`LocalRulesParams`] into the engine `RuleSet` for the
96	/// chosen variant. The picker → URL → page flow is the only call site.
97	pub fn build_rule_set(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> RuleSet {
98	    match variant {
99	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
100	            if params.strict {
101	                RuleSet::xiangqi()
102	            } else {
103	                RuleSet::xiangqi_casual()
104	            }
105	        }
106	        Variant::Banqi => match params.seed {
107	            Some(seed) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(params.house, seed),
108	            None => RuleSet::banqi(params.house),
109	        },
110	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => RuleSet::three_kingdom(),
111	    }
112	}
113	
114	fn parse_house_csv(s: &str) -> HouseRules {
115	    let mut out = HouseRules::empty();
116	    for tok in s.split(',').map(str::trim).filter(|t| !t.is_empty()) {
117	        if let Some(flag) = parse_house_token(tok) {
118	            out.insert(flag);
119	        }
120	    }
121	    out
122	}
123	
124	fn house_csv(rules: HouseRules) -> String {
125	    let mut parts: Vec<&'static str> = Vec::new();
126	    for (flag, tok) in HOUSE_TOKENS {
127	        if rules.contains(*flag) {
128	            parts.push(tok);
129	        }
130	    }
131	    parts.join(",")
132	}
133	
134	fn parse_house_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
135	    HOUSE_TOKENS
136	        .iter()
137	        .chain(HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES.iter())
138	        .find(|(_, t)| t.eq_ignore_ascii_case(tok))
139	        .map(|(f, _)| *f)
140	}
141	
142	fn parse_preset_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
143	    match tok.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
144	        "purist" => Some(PRESET_PURIST),
145	        "taiwan" => Some(PRESET_TAIWAN),
146	        "aggressive" => Some(PRESET_AGGRESSIVE),
147	        _ => None,
148	    }
149	}
150	
151	const HOUSE_TOKENS: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[
152	    (HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, "chain"),
153	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark"),
154	    (HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH, "rush"),
155	    (HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, "horse"),
156	    (HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE, "cannon"),
157	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE, "dark-trade"),
158	];
159	
160	/// Aliases recognised on parse only (older URLs / pre-rename snapshots).
161	/// Encoding uses the canonical `HOUSE_TOKENS` only.
162	const HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark-chain")];
163

166	    use super::*;

169	        move |k: &str| pairs.iter().find(|(pk, _)| *pk == k).map(|(_, v)| v.to_string())
170	    }
171	
172	    #[test]
173	    fn known_slugs_parse() {
174	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("xiangqi"), Some(Variant::Xiangqi)));
175	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("banqi"), Some(Variant::Banqi)));
176	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("three-kingdom"), Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi)));
177	    }
178	
179	    #[test]
180	    fn unknown_slug_returns_none() {
181	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("chess").is_none());
182	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("").is_none());

185	
186	    #[test]
187	    fn slug_round_trip() {
188	        for v in [Variant::Xiangqi, Variant::Banqi, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi] {
189	            assert_eq!(parse_variant_slug(variant_slug(v)), Some(v));
190	        }
191	    }
192

195	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[]));
196	        assert!(!p.strict);
197	        let r = build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
198	        assert!(r.xiangqi_allow_self_check);
199	    }

```text
1	//! Tiny text-input helpers for the host/lobby/create-room prompts.
2	//!
3	//! Deliberately not pulling in the `tui-input` crate — these inputs are
4	//! short ASCII strings (host:port, room id, password) and a 30-line helper
5	//! covers every keypress we care about.
6	
7	/// Append a printable char, capped at `max_len` bytes. Non-printables and
8	/// over-budget chars are silently dropped.
9	pub fn push_char(buf: &mut String, ch: char, max_len: usize) {
10	    if !ch.is_control() && buf.len() + ch.len_utf8() <= max_len {
11	        buf.push(ch);
12	    }
13	}
14	
15	/// Pop the last char (or no-op if empty).
16	pub fn backspace(buf: &mut String) {
17	    buf.pop();
18	}
19	
20	/// Mask a password for display: `••••••` (one `•` per char) up to 32, then
21	/// `…` for any extra. Empty becomes the placeholder `(none)`.
22	pub fn mask(pw: &str) -> String {
23	    if pw.is_empty() {
24	        return "(none)".into();
25	    }
26	    let n = pw.chars().count();
27	    if n <= 32 {
28	        "•".repeat(n)
29	    } else {
30	        format!("{}…", "•".repeat(31))
31	    }
32	}
33
```

562	    pub fn dispatch(&mut self, action: Action) {
563	        match action {
564	            Action::None => {}
565	            Action::ConfirmYes => {
566	                self.quit_confirm_open = false;
567	                self.should_quit = true;
568	            }
569	            Action::ConfirmNo => self.quit_confirm_open = false,
570	            Action::Quit => {
571	                if self.is_game_in_progress() {
572	                    self.quit_confirm_open = true;
573	                } else {
574	                    self.should_quit = true;
575	                }
576	            }
577	            Action::HelpToggle => self.help_open = !self.help_open,
578	            Action::RulesToggle => self.rules_open = !self.rules_open,
579	            Action::CapturedSortToggle => {
580	                self.captured_sort = self.captured_sort.toggled();
581	                let msg = format!("Captured sort: {}", self.captured_sort.label());
582	                match &mut self.screen {
583	                    Screen::Game(g) => g.last_msg = Some(msg),
584	                    Screen::Net(n) => n.last_msg = Some(msg),
585	                    _ => {}
586	                }
587	            }
588	            Action::NewGame => {
589	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Net(_)) {
590	                    // In Net mode, 'n' requests a rematch via the server
591	                    // instead of dropping the connection. Game must be over.
592	                    if let Screen::Net(n) = &mut self.screen {
593	                        if !n.role.map(|r| r.is_player()).unwrap_or(false) {
594	                            n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot request a rematch.".into());
595	                            return;
596	                        }
597	                        let status = n.last_view.as_ref().map(|v| v.status);
598	                        match status {
599	                            Some(GameStatus::Won { .. }) | Some(GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) => {
600	                                let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Rematch);
601	                                n.last_msg =
602	                                    Some("Rematch requested. Waiting for opponent…".into());
603	                            }
604	                            Some(GameStatus::Ongoing) => {
605	                                n.last_msg = Some(
606	                                    "'n' requests a rematch only after the game is over.".into(),
607	                                );
608	                            }
609	                            None => {
610	                                n.last_msg = Some("Not connected yet.".into());
611	                            }
612	                        }
613	                    }
614	                } else {
615	                    let style = self.style;
616	                    let use_color = self.use_color;
617	                    let observer = self.observer;
618	                    self.replace_preserving_prefs(AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer));
619	                }
620	            }
621	            Action::Back => self.dispatch_back(),
622	            Action::PickerUp | Action::PickerDown | Action::PickerSelect => match &self.screen {
623	                Screen::Picker(_) => self.dispatch_picker(action),
624	                Screen::Lobby(_) => self.dispatch_lobby(action),
625	                _ => {}
626	            },
627	            Action::LobbyCreate | Action::LobbyRefresh => {
628	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Lobby(_)) {
629	                    self.dispatch_lobby(action);
630	                }
631	            }
632	            Action::TextInput(_)
633	            | Action::TextBackspace
634	            | Action::FocusNext
635	            | Action::FocusPrev
636	            | Action::Submit => self.dispatch_text(action),
637	            Action::ChatStart => self.dispatch_chat_start(),
638	            Action::CoordStart(kind) => self.dispatch_coord_start(kind),
639	            Action::LobbyWatch => {
640	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Lobby(_)) {
641	                    self.dispatch_lobby_watch();
642	                }
643	            }
644	            _ => match &self.screen {
645	                Screen::Net(_) => self.dispatch_net(action),
646	                Screen::Game(_) => self.dispatch_game(action),
647	                Screen::Picker(_)
648	                | Screen::HostPrompt(_)
649	                | Screen::Lobby(_)
650	                | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => {}
651	            },
652	        }
653	        // Local moves go through dispatch_game / dispatch_coord_*, both of
654	        // which can land us on a terminal status. Re-check after every
655	        // dispatch so the confetti trigger fires reliably regardless of the
656	        // entry path. Net status is checked separately in `tick_net`.
657	        let cur = self.current_local_status();
658	        self.note_status_transition(cur, false);
659	    }

- **Entry point:** Selection calls `AppState::new_game(rules, ...)` (line 836–837) which constructs `Screen::Game` (app.rs:373)

### **2. CLI Parser Architecture**
- **Banqi flag parser:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs`, lines 202–233, function `build_banqi_rules()` — parses `--house chain,rush` and `--preset taiwan` into `HouseRules` bitflags, then calls `RuleSet::banqi()` or `RuleSet::banqi_with_seed()`
- **Subcommand args:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs`, lines 99–122, enum `Cmd` with variants `Xiangqi { strict }` and `Banqi { preset, house, seed }`
- **RuleSet factories called:** `RuleSet::xiangqi()`, `RuleSet::xiangqi_casual()`, `RuleSet::banqi(flags)`, `RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(flags, seed)` (defined in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/rules/mod.rs`, lines 56–80)

### **3. Preset + HouseRules Flags**
- **Presets:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/rules/house.rs`, lines 30–41:
  - `PRESET_PURIST` = empty (no flags)
  - `PRESET_TAIWAN` = `CHAIN_CAPTURE | CHARIOT_RUSH`
  - `PRESET_AGGRESSIVE` = `CHAIN_CAPTURE | DARK_CAPTURE | CHARIOT_RUSH | HORSE_DIAGONAL`
- **HouseRules flags (6 toggles):** lines 12–26 of house.rs: `CHAIN_CAPTURE`, `DARK_CAPTURE`, `CHARIOT_RUSH`, `HORSE_DIAGONAL`, `CANNON_FAST_MOVE`, `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE`
- **Token parser (already exists!):** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs`, lines 114–149, functions `parse_house_csv()`, `parse_house_token()` — parses "chain,dark,rush,horse,cannon,dark-trade" to flags; **REUSABLE** — could copy to chess-tui or call from `chess_core` (currently embedded in web client)

### **4. TUI Keymap Dispatcher + InputMode**
- **Dispatcher:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs`, line 562, `pub fn dispatch(&mut self, action: Action)` branches on `InputMode::`
- **InputMode enum:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/input.rs`, lines 9–14 — variants: `Picker`, `Lobby`, `Text`, `Game`
- **Input mode resolution:** app.rs:545–558, `input_mode()` method returns current mode based on screen type (e.g., `Screen::Game(g) if g.coord_input.is_some()` → `InputMode::Text`)
- **Keymap entry point:** main.rs:289, `from_key(k, mode, quit_confirm)` reads key + mode, emits `Action` (lines 82–144 in input.rs)
- **Coord input pattern:** Game mode hijacks `InputMode::Text` via `CoordInputState` (app.rs:96–102); dispatch_text() (lines 1009–1017) routes to `dispatch_coord_text_game()` when `coord_input.is_some()`
- **Pattern for new "rules input" mode:** Add new `CoordInputState`-like struct → set it when user presses a trigger key (e.g. `c` for "custom") → `input_mode()` detects it and returns `InputMode::Text` → `dispatch_text()` reads buffer + applies flags

### **5. Picker Screen Nesting / Sub-screens**
- **Current state:** Picker is flat — no nested sub-screens, no recursion. `PickerEntry::ConnectToServer` transitions to `HostPrompt` screen (entirely separate screen enum), not a sub-menu
- **Screen enum:** app.rs:202–209 lists 6 top-level variants: `Picker`, `Game`, `Net`, `HostPrompt`, `Lobby`, `CreateRoom` — **no nesting**
- **Minimal delta for "Custom rules…" sub-screen:** Add `PickerCustomRules(CustomRulesView)` variant to `Screen` enum → transition from Picker when user selects it → handle `dispatch_picker()` → on selection push back to Picker with the chosen flags, or transition to Game. **Easiest pattern:** stay in Picker, add overlay/sub-render like the rules/quit-confirm overlays (app.rs:41–46, ui.rs:41–46)

### **6. Ratatui Widgets in Use**
- **Current:** `Block`, `Borders`, `Paragraph`, `Wrap` only (ui.rs:12)
- **List widget:** NOT currently used anywhere in TUI (grep found zero imports of ratatui::widgets::List)
- **Checkbox prior art:** NONE in this repo — would need custom rendering
- **Pattern for checkbox rows:** `draw_picker()` already uses hand-rolled cursor display (`"▶ "` vs `"  "` prefix, line 74); extending this to toggle flags with checkboxes or `[x]`/`[ ]` is trivial with Span/Paragraph (no widget library needed)

### **7. Banner & Title Art**
- **File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/banner.rs`
- **Render in picker:** ui.rs:55–69, `draw_picker()` calls `banner::art(BannerKind::AppTitle, style)` and renders rows as styled Spans; falls back to "chess-tui" text if terminal too narrow
- **Current: Keep the banner above the list** — it's already integrated into the same `lines: Vec<Line>` buffer as the menu items (ui.rs:50, 70–72, then loop 73–81)

---

**Summary:** The picker is a simple flat list with cursor at app.rs:79–81 `PickerView { cursor }`. Selection dispatches via `entry.rules()` and `AppState::new_game()`. A "custom rules" mode would either (a) add a new `Screen` variant for a sub-menu, or (b) reuse the `InputMode::Text` pattern (hijack text keymap + buffer) to enter a mini "rules-input mode" while staying in Picker, then resolve flags and transition. The web client's `parse_house_csv()` + `HOUSE_TOKENS` parser is drop-in reusable code for tokenizing flag strings.

## Summary

**1. Location:** 
- **File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs`
- **Function:** `draw_rules_overlay()` (lines 1565–1591)
- **Constant:** `RULES_LINES` (lines 1641–1668)

**2. Text is STATIC:**
The overlay uses a hardcoded constant `RULES_LINES` with no branching on `Variant`, `RuleSet`, or `xiangqi_allow_self_check`. The overlay is identical regardless of game mode or rules enabled.

**3. No house-rules status shown:**
The text mentions house rules exist ("chain-capture (連吃), chariot-rush (車衝)") but **does NOT** indicate which are enabled. Line 1665 explicitly says "Other house rules are P1 TODO." No seed/shuffle info is shown.

**4. Rendered text excerpt:**
```
"• House rules (toggleable presets): chain-capture (連吃), chariot-rush"
"  (車衝). Other house rules are P1 TODO."
"• Standard: any move that leaves your general capturable is illegal."
"  Casual mode (--allow-self-check / picker entry) lifts that filter;"
```

**5. RuleSet accessibility:**
`GameView` (accessible at render time via `app.screen`) contains `state: GameState`, which has a public `rules: Option<RuleSet>` field (app.rs line 136). **Making the overlay dynamic is straightforward** — just pass the `RuleSet` to `draw_rules_overlay()` instead of `_style`.

- **兩個都做 (推薦)** - 樹狀選單做主要 UX (新手友善),args 輸入框作為快速 power-user 通道。實作上樹狀畫面也可內嵌 args 預覽行。

**進遊戲後的 rules overlay (按 r) 要怎麼跟著實際規則走?**

- **只列出已啟用的房規** - 只在通用說明後面加一行 'Enabled house rules: chain, rush'。簡潔但看不到哪些沒開。
- **暫時不動** - 只做 picker 改造,rules overlay 保持靜態。

**Custom rules 子畫面要不要也支援 xiangqi (strict / casual 切換)?**

- **只做 banqi** - xiangqi 維持兩個 preset 列項;Custom rules 只給 banqi 用 (因為 banqi 才有 6 個旗標 + seed)。最小範圍。

```md
# TUI menu UX — tree-style custom-rules picker + dynamic rules overlay

## Context

The chess-tui main menu currently exposes only fixed presets (`PickerEntry::ALL`, `clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs:27-77`):
xiangqi standard/strict, banqi purist/Taiwan/aggressive, online, quit. There is no way for a TUI user to mix individual house-rule flags or pick a deterministic seed without dropping out and re-running with CLI args (`--house chain,rush --preset taiwan --seed 42`, parsed at `main.rs:202-233`).

Worse, once in-game, the rules overlay (`r`) shows a static `RULES_LINES` constant (`ui.rs:1641-1668`) that mentions house rules in the abstract ("chain-capture (連吃), chariot-rush (車衝). Other house rules are P1 TODO.") but never tells you **which flags are actually on** for the current game, nor the banqi seed.

This change adds:

1. A **tree-style "Custom rules…" sub-screen** reachable from the picker (one entry per variant), with cursor-navigated radio-buttons (preset), checkbox rows (six house-rule flags, `Space` toggles), and a seed text input. UX modelled on Image #4 (skill-install screen). Banqi gets all six flags + seed; xiangqi gets a Standard/Casual radio.
2. A **rules-aware overlay**: `draw_rules_overlay` becomes dynamic — it takes the live `RuleSet` (and seed if known) and prepends a `[x] / [ ]` summary of the flags that are active for the running game, before the existing generic rules text.

The CLI `--house`/`--preset`/`--seed` path stays untouched (still useful for scripts and `make play-local`); the new screen is purely additive.

## Files to modify

| File | What changes |
|---|---|
| `clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs` | New `Screen::CustomRules(CustomRulesView)` variant; new struct `CustomRulesView`; new `PickerEntry::CustomXiangqi` + `CustomBanqi`; `dispatch_picker` routes the new entries; `dispatch_custom_rules` handles cursor / toggle / start; `input_mode()` returns `InputMode::CustomRules` (or `Text` when seed-edit active) |
| `clients/chess-tui/src/input.rs` | Add `InputMode::CustomRules` variant; `from_key` adds rows for it: `j`/`k`/arrows = `CursorMove`, `Space` = `Toggle`, `h`/`l` = `CycleLeft`/`CycleRight`, `Enter` = `Confirm`, `Esc`/`q` = `Back` |
| `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` | New `draw_custom_rules(f, area, view, style)`; modify `draw_rules_overlay(f, area, rules, variant, seed, style)` signature to accept the live `RuleSet`/variant and build the `[x]/[ ]` summary block above `RULES_LINES`; call sites updated to pass `app.current_rules()` and `app.current_seed()` |
| `clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs` | (Optional) Extract `build_banqi_rules` so `CustomRulesView::to_rule_set` can reuse the same flag→`HouseRules` mapping; otherwise inline the conversion — both fine, this is small code |
| `clients/chess-tui/src/banner.rs` | No changes — `BannerKind::AppTitle` reused above the new sub-screen |

No `chess-core` changes. No protocol bump. No new ratatui widgets — re-uses the `Paragraph` + `Line` + `Span` pattern already in `draw_picker` (`ui.rs:49-85`).

## Design

### 1. Picker entry points

Append two entries to `PickerEntry::ALL` (`app.rs:27-77`), placed right after the last banqi preset:

\```rust
PickerEntry::CustomXiangqi,   // label: "Xiangqi (象棋) — custom rules…"
PickerEntry::CustomBanqi,     // label: "Banqi (暗棋) — custom rules…"
\```

`PickerEntry::rules()` is unchanged for the existing six entries; the two new ones don't return a `RuleSet` directly — instead `dispatch_picker` (`app.rs:814-845`) treats them specially and pushes `Screen::CustomRules(CustomRulesView::new_xiangqi())` / `::new_banqi(default_preset)`.

### 2. `CustomRulesView` state

\```rust
pub struct CustomRulesView {
    variant: VariantKind,            // Xiangqi | Banqi (local enum, not chess_core::Variant — we don't need ThreeKingdom here)
    cursor: usize,                   // index into rows()
    // Xiangqi:
    xiangqi_strict: bool,            // false = casual (default, matches current Xiangqi entry)
    // Banqi:
    banqi_preset: BanqiPreset,       // Purist | Taiwan | Aggressive | Custom
    banqi_flags: HouseRules,         // syncs with banqi_preset; switches to Custom on manual toggle
    banqi_seed: String,              // user-typed seed; empty = thread_rng()
    seed_editing: bool,              // true when cursor on Seed row AND user pressed Enter to start editing
}
\```

`rows()` returns a `Vec<CustomRulesRow>` so cursor navigation and rendering share the layout:

\```rust
enum CustomRulesRow {
    Header(&'static str),     // not selectable
    Preset(usize),            // index in preset list
    FlagToggle(HouseRules),   // single-bit flag
    SeedInput,
    StartButton,
}
\```

`Header` rows are skipped during cursor moves (`dispatch_custom_rules` advances past them).

### 3. RuleSet construction on Enter

`CustomRulesView::to_rule_set(self) -> RuleSet`:
- Xiangqi: `RuleSet::xiangqi()` if `xiangqi_strict`, else `RuleSet::xiangqi_casual()`.
- Banqi: parse `banqi_seed.trim()` — empty → `RuleSet::banqi(banqi_flags)`; non-empty digit string → `RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(banqi_flags, parsed_seed)`; non-numeric → fall through to a `last_msg` error in the view (don't start the game).

Re-uses existing factories in `crates/chess-core/src/rules/mod.rs:56-80`. Same `HouseRules` flags as `house.rs:12-26`. No new public API in `chess-core`.

### 4. Picker → screen transition

`dispatch_picker` (`app.rs:814-845`) extends:

\```rust
PickerEntry::CustomXiangqi => self.screen = Screen::CustomRules(CustomRulesView::new_xiangqi()),
PickerEntry::CustomBanqi   => self.screen = Screen::CustomRules(CustomRulesView::new_banqi()),
\```

`Esc` from `Screen::CustomRules` returns to `Screen::Picker(PickerView { cursor: <last picker cursor> })` — store the picker cursor on `CustomRulesView` so we restore it. Same pattern as how `HostPrompt` returns to `Picker` today.

### 5. Rendering

`draw_custom_rules(f, area, view, style)` in `ui.rs`. Layout (top to bottom inside one centred `Block::bordered` like the rules overlay):

\```
┌ Custom Banqi rules ─────────────────────────────┐
│                                                 │
│   Preset                                        │
│   ▶ ( ) Purist                                  │
│     (•) Taiwan                                  │
│     ( ) Aggressive                              │
│     ( ) Custom                                  │
│                                                 │
│   House rules (Space to toggle)                 │
│     [x] chain      連吃                         │
│     [ ] dark       暗吃                         │
│     [x] rush       車衝                         │
│     [ ] horse      馬斜                         │
│     [ ] cannon     砲快                         │
│     [ ] dark-trade                              │
│                                                 │
│   Seed: [ 42         ]   (空白=隨機)            │
│                                                 │
│   [ Start ]                                     │
│                                                 │
│ [Enter] Start  [Space] Toggle  [Esc] Back       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\```

Uses the existing `Paragraph` + bordered `Block` pattern from `draw_picker` (`ui.rs:49-85`) and `draw_rules_overlay` (`ui.rs:1565-1591`). Cursor row gets `▶` prefix + `style::Style::accent`. Disabled (header) rows just plain.

### 6. Dynamic rules overlay

Change signature: `fn draw_rules_overlay(f, area, rules: &RuleSet, seed: Option<u64>, style: &StyleSet)` (was `_style`-only).

Build a prelude `Vec<Line>`:

\```
Banqi 暗棋 — current rules
  [x] chain-capture     連吃
  [ ] dark-capture      暗吃
  [x] chariot-rush      車衝
  [ ] horse-diagonal    馬斜
  [ ] cannon-fast       砲快
  [ ] dark-trade
  Seed: 42 (deterministic)

\```

For xiangqi, replace the 6-row block with a single row: `Standard self-check` or `Casual (allow self-check)` based on `rules.xiangqi_allow_self_check`. The seed row is omitted for xiangqi.

Then append `RULES_LINES` (the existing static body) so generic xiangqi/banqi explanations are still there.

`GameView` exposes `state.rules: Option<RuleSet>` (`app.rs:136`). For Net, `NetView` already holds `rules: RuleSet` from the server `Welcome` payload (per CLAUDE.md "v3 spectator + chat" gotcha). Add a tiny `app.current_rules() -> Option<&RuleSet>` accessor so `draw_rules_overlay` callers (Game and Net) share one path. Seed is **not** known on the client for Net mode (server doesn't ship it) — pass `None` and the seed row is omitted; for Local mode pass the seed the user typed (store it on `GameView` alongside `rules`, or re-derive from `RuleSet` if a seed accessor is added later — the simplest is to remember it on construction in `new_game`).

### 7. Web parity reference (do **not** modify)

The web client's `parse_house_csv` (`clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs:114-149`) already enumerates the same six tokens (`chain | dark | rush | horse | cannon | dark-trade`). Use the same labels and the same Chinese subtitles in the TUI rows so the two UIs stay coherent. Per CLAUDE.md gotcha (`backlog/promote-client-shared.md`), do **not** promote a shared crate yet — duplicate the few label strings.

## Implementation order

1. **Add `Custom*` picker entries + `Screen::CustomRules` plumbing** (no rendering yet) — verify `dispatch_picker` routes correctly with a `dbg!` and `Esc` returns to picker.
2. **Add `InputMode::CustomRules` + keymap rows in `input.rs`** — verify `j`/`k`/`Space`/`Enter`/`Esc` reach the dispatcher.
3. **Implement `dispatch_custom_rules`**: cursor move, preset cycle, flag toggle (which flips preset to `Custom`), seed digit append/backspace, `Confirm` on `StartButton` builds `RuleSet` and calls existing `new_game` (`app.rs:373`).
4. **Render `draw_custom_rules`** — start with plain text rows, add cursor + checkbox glyphs.
5. **Make rules overlay dynamic** — change signature, build the per-variant `[x]/[ ]` block, prepend to `RULES_LINES`, update both Game and Net call sites.
6. **Persist seed on `GameView`** for the Local case so the overlay can show it (Net stays `None`).

## Verification

\```bash
# Build + lint
cargo fmt --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo build -p chess-tui

# Picker → Custom Banqi → toggle chain + rush, set seed=42, Enter
cargo run -p chess-tui
# In picker: arrow down to "Banqi (暗棋) — custom rules…", Enter
# In custom screen: Space on chain, Space on rush, k to Seed, type "42", k to Start, Enter
# In game: press `r` — overlay should show:
#   [x] chain-capture     連吃
#   [ ] dark-capture      暗吃
#   [x] chariot-rush      車衝
#   ...
#   Seed: 42 (deterministic)

# Custom Xiangqi → Casual → Enter; press `r` should show "Casual (allow self-check)"
# Custom Xiangqi → Standard → Enter; should show "Standard self-check"

# Esc from custom screen returns to picker with cursor where it was
# Determinism: re-run with seed=42, banqi initial flip layout should match

# CLI args path still works (regression check)
cargo run -p chess-tui -- banqi --preset taiwan --seed 42
cargo run -p chess-tui -- xiangqi --strict

# Net mode: rules overlay still works (no seed shown)
make play-local VARIANT=banqi
# In one of the client panes press `r` — flag list reflects what server picked

# Workspace
cargo test --workspace
cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-core
\```

No new perft / engine tests needed — this is pure client UX. If we want regression coverage, add a tiny `app.rs` unit test asserting `CustomRulesView::to_rule_set` produces the expected `RuleSet` for each preset + flag combination (mirrors web's `routes.rs` tests).

```

1. **Add `Custom*` picker entries + `Screen::CustomRules` plumbing** (no rendering yet) — verify `dispatch_picker` routes correctly with a `dbg!` and `Esc` returns to picker.
2. **Add `InputMode::CustomRules` + keymap rows in `input.rs`** — verify `j`/`k`/`Space`/`Enter`/`Esc` reach the dispatcher.
3. **Implement `dispatch_custom_rules`**: cursor move, preset cycle, flag toggle (which flips preset to `Custom`), seed digit append/backspace, `Confirm` on `StartButton` builds `RuleSet` and calls existing `new_game` (`app.rs:373`).
4. **Render `draw_custom_rules`** — start with plain text rows, add cursor + checkbox glyphs.
5. **Make rules overlay dynamic** — change signature, build the per-variant `[x]/[ ]` block, prepend to `RULES_LINES`, update both Game and Net call sites.
6. **Persist seed on `GameView`** for the Local case so the overlay can show it (Net stays `None`).

## Verification

```bash
# Build + lint
cargo fmt --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo build -p chess-tui

# Picker → Custom Banqi → toggle chain + rush, set seed=42, Enter
cargo run -p chess-tui
# In picker: arrow down to "Banqi (暗棋) — custom rules…", Enter
# In custom screen: Space on chain, Space on rush, k to Seed, type "42", k to Start, Enter
# In game: press `r` — overlay should show:
#   [x] chain-capture     連吃
#   [ ] dark-capture      暗吃
#   [x] chariot-rush      車衝
#   ...
#   Seed: 42 (deterministic)

# Custom Xiangqi → Casual → Enter; press `r` should show "Casual (allow self-check)"
# Custom Xiangqi → Standard → Enter; should show "Standard self-check"

# Esc from custom screen returns to picker with cursor where it was
# Determinism: re-run with seed=42, banqi initial flip layout should match

# CLI args path still works (regression check)
cargo run -p chess-tui -- banqi --preset taiwan --seed 42
cargo run -p chess-tui -- xiangqi --strict

# Net mode: rules overlay still works (no seed shown)
make play-local VARIANT=banqi
# In one of the client panes press `r` — flag list reflects what server picked

# Workspace
cargo test --workspace
cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-core
```

No new perft / engine tests needed — this is pure client UX. If we want regression coverage, add a tiny `app.rs` unit test asserting `CustomRulesView::to_rule_set` produces the expected `RuleSet` for each preset + flag combination (mirrors web's `routes.rs` tests).

253	/// Sort order for the sidebar "captured pieces" panel. `Time` keeps
254	/// the chronological order returned by `GameState::captured_pieces()`;
255	/// `Rank` re-sorts each side's row by piece value (largest first).
256	/// User toggles via `g` in `Game` mode and `--captured-sort` on the
257	/// CLI; preserved across screen transitions like the FX prefs.
258	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Default)]
259	pub enum CapturedSort {
260	    #[default]
261	    Time,
262	    Rank,
263	}
264	
265	impl CapturedSort {
266	    pub fn toggled(self) -> Self {
267	        match self {
268	            CapturedSort::Time => CapturedSort::Rank,
269	            CapturedSort::Rank => CapturedSort::Time,
270	        }
271	    }
272	
273	    pub fn label(self) -> &'static str {
274	        match self {
275	            CapturedSort::Time => "time",
276	            CapturedSort::Rank => "rank",
277	        }
278	    }
279	}
280	
281	/// Rank-ordering for `CapturedSort::Rank`. Larger value = stronger piece
282	/// (General > Advisor > Elephant > Chariot > Horse > Cannon > Soldier).
283	/// Used by both the TUI panel and a future shared client crate.
284	pub fn piece_rank_value(kind: PieceKind) -> u8 {
285	    match kind {
286	        PieceKind::General => 6,
287	        PieceKind::Advisor => 5,
288	        PieceKind::Elephant => 4,
289	        PieceKind::Chariot => 3,
290	        PieceKind::Horse => 2,
291	        PieceKind::Cannon => 1,
292	        PieceKind::Soldier => 0,
293	    }
294	}
295	
296	/// Sort `pieces` (chronological order from the engine) into a per-side
297	/// view appropriate for the sidebar panel. Returns `(red, black)`.
298	pub fn split_and_sort_captured(pieces: &[Piece], sort: CapturedSort) -> (Vec<Piece>, Vec<Piece>) {
299	    let mut red: Vec<Piece> = pieces.iter().filter(|p| p.side == Side::RED).copied().collect();
300	    let mut black: Vec<Piece> = pieces.iter().filter(|p| p.side == Side::BLACK).copied().collect();
301	    if sort == CapturedSort::Rank {
302	        red.sort_by(|a, b| piece_rank_value(b.kind).cmp(&piece_rank_value(a.kind)));
303	        black.sort_by(|a, b| piece_rank_value(b.kind).cmp(&piece_rank_value(a.kind)));
304	    }
305	    (red, black)
306	}
307
